Position Overview

Our client, a leading global pharmaceutical organization, is seeking a highly organized and proactive Project Coordinator III to support strategic initiatives within the Quality, Medical & Governance (QM&G) organization. This individual will serve as a key operational partner to senior leadership, providing project coordination, executive support, meeting and event management, and cross-functional collaboration across Research & Development stakeholders.

The ideal candidate combines strong project coordination experience with exceptional organizational skills, executive-level professionalism, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Experience supporting pharmaceutical, biotech, clinical operations, medical affairs, or R&D organizations is highly preferred.

Key Responsibilities

Project Coordination & Operations

Provide project management and operational support for cross-functional initiatives and strategic programs.
Coordinate project activities including meeting scheduling, agenda development, action item tracking, status updates, and follow-up communications.
Maintain project documentation, participation rosters, and shared repositories.
Create and manage SharePoint sites, Microsoft Teams channels, OneNote workspaces, and other collaboration tools to support project teams.
Monitor project deliverables and timelines while escalating risks or issues when appropriate.
Support governance processes and stakeholder communications.

Coordinate logistics including venue management, catering, travel arrangements, communications, and meeting materials.
Ensure activities comply with company financial, ethical, and procurement guidelines.
Manage event budgets and vendor relationships while maintaining cost efficiency.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Partner with Procurement, Finance, suppliers, business managers, and project teams to support contractual agreements and project advancement.
Build strong working relationships across R&D, Medical Affairs, Quality, and other business functions.
Maintain confidentiality while supporting sensitive business initiatives.

Strong project coordination, organizational, and administrative support experience.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to manage multiple priorities with minimal supervision.
Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
Comfortable interacting with senior leaders and executive-level stakeholders.
Experience handling confidential and sensitive information.

new_jobs.title: Project Coordinator II
new_jobs.description:
Job Overview

B Comm Constructors LLC was formed in 2013 as a small telecom construction and repair company, with the aspiration to become a comprehensive firm capable of design and engineering as well. In 2022, B Comm joined Future Infrastructure, a Primoris Company, who is a leading communications infrastructure specialty contractor offering program management, engineering, construction, and maintenance services to the leading communications companies across U.S. markets.

The Project Coordinator will collaborate with in-field management to ensure data is extracted from the field and translated into company record for deliverables that reflect accuracy, quality of work, compliance with standards and specifications, and consistency. This role reports to the Project Manager and senior operations leadership. Must live near, Austin TX.

Responsibilities

Work with the Project Management Team, Construction Team, and Splice Team as needed to ultimately provide support including updating and maintaining milestone forecast, project documentation creation and uploads, vendor PO requests and municipal permit applications.
Attend all in-person and virtual project meeting requests with Customers and internal teams.
Align communications with field inspectors, Customer Agents, City Staff / Regulatory Agency Representatives
Provide Project Coordination, Oversight, and Logistics Management
Collection of construction close-out information and organization into a presentable format for delivery to the Customer in a timely manner, calling out and flagging potential risks, delays, blockers, challenges, etc. Report all hindrances to Project Management Team for mitigation and risk planning.
Creation and continual update of Construction and Splicing Redlines including remediation with field crews as needed for billing.
Data collection, management, and coordination with the team as required for project closeout and client specific deliverables.
Collection and processing of daily production report submittals from Construction Supervisors and subcontractors.
Translation of in-field production activities into central billing units, and reporting those units as required to the Customer in the Customer’s prescribed format or proprietary software.
Creating reports of production, sharing metrics daily with team, tracking goals and milestones, and informing of remaining quantities and financial support.
Analyze prints and photos for accuracy and incorporate into reports, documents, and custom maps including taking remediation photos

Qualifications

Experience with based reporting tools , design platforms and unique portal billing a plus
Relevant work experience in a similar role with required technical skills may suffice.
Ability to multi-task and thrive under pressure in a fast-paced work environment
Self-motivated and self-directed including possessing strong communication skills, verbal and written with ability to train others for team success
Advanced Microsoft Office skills with the ability to perform in-depth analysis with many different types of data including Excel and SmartSheets, PowerPoint, Bluebean, Adobe.
Ability to build strong relationships with team members, vendors, and customers

Work Environment

Office, Client Space

Physical Demands

Prolonged periods of sitting in an office setting

Other Duties

Primoris Services Corporation (Primoris) is ranked in the top 10% of the prestigious Top 600 Specialty Contractors List by Engineering News Record (ENR), having built projects throughout the United States as well as Latin America, South and Central America, and Southeast Asia.

Company Overview

Primoris is a leading communications contractor with years of knowledge, skills, and experience in multiple areas of expertise, including outside plant construction in both fiber and copper environments as well as fiber splicing and maintenance.
